description: A period of seven days; the basic cycle of work and rest in many cultures. 'Phaeno' names the weekly rhythm—days of activity punctuated by rest, the pulse of communal time.
sound_symbolism: The word cycles through. 'Pha' /ɸa/ begins with breath, the cycle starting. The vowel 'e' opens to the span. Then 'no' grounds at completion, week ending.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'ta phaeno' (one week). Longer than a day, shorter than a month. Common unit for planning and scheduling.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Weekly practice builds habit—the regularity that transforms occasional effort into established path.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Weekly rhythms organized labor—market days, rest days, patterns that structured community life.
